summary:
Ptosis, muscle fatigue, and preserved pupillary reflexes are classic signs of myasthenia gravis. Absent deep tendon reflexes are not observed; instead, DTRs are typically normal in this condition.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only two sentences and focuses exclusively on clinically significant aspects related directly to identifying which feature is NOT associated with myasthenia gravis.) 

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{C}}

The correct answer is **C** because it represents an abnormality (absent DTRs) that contradicts established clinical findings — unlike other options such as ptosis or muscle fatigability—which are well-documented manifestations of myasthenia gravis. Myasthenia gravis presents with variable weakness due to impaired neuromuscular transmission but maintains intact reflexes including those at joints tested by deep tendon percussion.
